sglang.title: Refactor speculative algorithm registry.
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Prev code is overly complex and provides no practical benefit for the current design.

Speculative algorithms are static in practice and do not require a dynamic registry.
The set of speculative algorithms is small and well-defined, and can be safely hardcoded.
The lock is unnecessary, as there is n

sglang.title: Fusing RMSNormTP in minimax_m2
sglang.author: rogeryoungh
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR merges q_norm and k_norm into qk_norm. Each RMSNormTP contains 3 kernels: summation, all_reduce, and the remaining norm operation. This implementation combines 3x2=6 steps into 3 steps.
Previous: 6 kernels were launched.

Now: Only 3 kernel is launched.


Modifications

Accuracy T

sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Previously, sgl-kernel’s make build hard-coded MAX_JOBS=$(nproc) and CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=$(nproc), so even if you passed MAX_JOBS=2, it would be overridden and the build would still use all CPU cores.
Now you can override these parameters when running make build: MAX_JOBS, CMAKE_BU
